description: Common fault patterns for MacBook Air 13 M1 (A2337, 820-02016). Based on M1 architecture patterns and known failure modes.
WARNING: M1 board. Faults listed here are board-specific. Do NOT apply A2681 (M2) fault patterns directly. PMU: SERA=Master (U8100), SIMETRA=Slave (U7700).
safan_data_status: No A2337-specific records found in safan_diagnostics_parsed.json. The a_numbers and board_numbers fields are empty in all records. QA validation against schematic not possible with current Safan data.

common_faults.name: No power - CD3217 not negotiating
common_faults.symptom: Board draws no current or minimal current (<10mA) at 5V. No PPBUS_AON.
common_faults.root_cause: CD3217 (UF400/UF500) port controller failure or SPI ROM corruption
diagnosis_steps: Check CC1/CC2 lines for continuity (page 55/56)
Check SPI ROM (SPI_UPC0/SPI_UPC1) for corruption
Measure VBUS pass-through on CD3217 output
Check Burnside Bridge (UF000/UF100) for shorts dragging VBUS
common_faults.fix: Replace UF400 or UF500 (CD3217). If SPI ROM corrupted, reprogram or replace.

common_faults.name: No power - Charger IC fault
common_faults.symptom: 5V present at VBUS but no PPBUS_AON. Board draws 50-200mA at 5V but does not boot.
common_faults.root_cause: Charger IC (U5200) failure or battery SYS_DETECT not triggered
diagnosis_steps: Check PPBUS_AON at bulk cap - should be ~12V with battery
Check CHGR_AUX_OK signal (page 24)
Check BMU connector J5100 SYS_DETECT (page 22)
Check for shorts on PPBUS_AON bulk caps (diode mode)
common_faults.fix: If SYS_DETECT missing: check J5100 connector and gasket circuit. If charger IC dead: replace U5200.

common_faults.name: No power - PP3V8_AON short
common_faults.symptom: PPBUS_AON present but PP3V8_AON missing or low. High current draw on PPBUS_AON.
common_faults.root_cause: Short circuit on PP3V8_AON distribution (VDDMAIN or VODMAN branch)
diagnosis_steps: Measure diode mode on PP3V8_AON bulk caps
Check PPVAON_FAULT_L - will be low if U5700 detects overload
Use thermal imaging at low voltage to find hot component
Systematically lift capacitors on PP3V8_AON branches to isolate short
Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN caps first (common short location)
common_faults.fix: Replace shorted capacitor or IC pulling PP3V8_AON low. Common: capacitor failure on VDDMAIN branch.

common_faults.name: No power - U5700 SVR AON failure
common_faults.symptom: PPBUS_AON present, PPVAON_PWR_EN asserted, but PP3V8_AON missing. No short detected.
common_faults.root_cause: U5700 SVR AON controller IC failure
diagnosis_steps: Verify PPVAON_PWR_EN is high
Verify PPVAON_FAULT_L is not pulled low
Check U5700 switching waveform at phase outputs
Check inductor continuity on SVR AON output

common_faults.name: No boot - PMU not starting
common_faults.symptom: PP3V8_AON present at 3.8V but PMU LDO outputs missing. No PP5V_S2 or PP3V3_S2.
diagnosis_steps: Check SERA LDO 9 output (~1.8V) - if missing, SERA (U8100) may be dead
Check SIMETRA LDO 9 output (~1.8V) - if missing, SIMETRA (U7700) may be dead
Check for shorts on PMU LDO output caps
Thermal imaging to find hot PMU
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY signal
common_faults.fix: Replace failed PMU IC. U8100 (SERA/Master) or U7700 (SIMETRA/Slave).

common_faults.name: No boot - PP5V_S2 missing
common_faults.symptom: PMU rails present but PP5V_S2 missing. Board partially powers on but does not boot.
common_faults.root_cause: UC320 5V buck converter failure or enable signal missing
diagnosis_steps: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN - should be asserted by PMU GPIO
Check for short on PP5V_S2 output (diode mode on output caps)
Check UC320 inductor for continuity
If shorted: check PP5V_S2SW_IPD load switch for downstream short
common_faults.fix: If P5VS2_PWR_EN missing: trace PMU GPIO. If output shorted: find and fix short. If UC320 dead: replace UC320.

common_faults.name: No boot - PP3V3_S2 missing
common_faults.symptom: PMU rails present but PP3V3_S2 missing.
common_faults.root_cause: XMC710 3V3 buck converter failure or enable signal missing
diagnosis_steps: Check P3V3S2_PWR_EN gating logic (page 37)
Check for short on PP3V3_S2 output
Check XMC710 inductor continuity
common_faults.fix: Fix P3V3S2_PWR_EN path or replace XMC710.

common_faults.name: USB-C port not working (one port)
common_faults.symptom: One USB-C port does not charge or data. Other port works fine.
common_faults.root_cause: CD3217 port controller fault on affected port, or connector damage
diagnosis_steps: Check CC1/CC2 on affected port for shorts/opens
Check SBU1/SBU2 for shorts
Check VBUS for dead short
Inspect connector physically for bent/broken pins
Check Burnside Bridge (UF000/UF100) for related fault
common_faults.fix: Replace connector if physical damage. Replace CD3217 (UF400 or UF500) if controller fault.

common_faults.name: No display - backlight fault
common_faults.symptom: Board boots (fan spin, chime) but no display. External display may work.
common_faults.root_cause: BEN (DP600) backlight boost converter failure or display connector issue
diagnosis_steps: Check display connector (page 67) for physical damage
Check PPBUS_AONSW_LCD load switch output
Check DP600 (BEN) input and output (page 69)
Check display power sequencer UP700/UP705/UP715 (page 68)
Try known-good display cable/panel
common_faults.fix: Replace DP600 (BEN), display connector, or display cable. If UP700/UP705 sequencer fault: replace sequencer IC.

common_faults.name: WiFi/Bluetooth not working
common_faults.symptom: Board boots and works but no WiFi or Bluetooth detected in macOS.
common_faults.root_cause: Rasputin WiFi/BT module failure or antenna connection issue
diagnosis_steps: Check WiFi/BT module power rails (page 60)
Check antenna connections (page 61)
Check for physical damage to Rasputin module
Check PCIe connection to SoC (page 9)
common_faults.fix: Reseat antenna cables. If module fault: replace Rasputin WiFi/BT module.

common_faults.name: SSD not detected
common_faults.symptom: Board boots to DFU/recovery but cannot see internal storage.
common_faults.root_cause: NAND flash failure, NAND power rail missing, or SoC NAND controller issue
diagnosis_steps: Check NAND power rails on pages 62-63
Check NAND clock and data connections
Check for physical damage to NAND packages
Note: M1 has integrated SSD controller in SoC - NAND is soldered on MLB
common_faults.fix: NAND replacement on M1 is extremely difficult (paired to SoC). If NAND fault confirmed, board may be unrepairable for data recovery.
